name: rasa.module.notes
description: Portable institutional-memory workflow: the three-tier working-note -> durable decision-log -> canon promotion pipeline that domain-writer, domain-code, and this workspace each reinvented independently. Human-authored, git-versioned markdown ledgers, read start-to-finish and ratified by a person — distinct from the kernel's similarity-recall memory (which module.ingest wraps). The one thing that varies per project — where a promoted decision lands and what the promotion criteria are — is delegated to a project-owned .claude/notes-canon.md.

rasa.note: The Element-layer institutional-memory module — the human-authored, git-versioned counterpart to the kernel's similarity-recall memory (which rasa.module.ingest wraps). Distilled from three independent RasaOS reimplementations of the SAME three-tier promotion pipeline: domain-writer (seed/ledgers/decisions.md -> bible canon, with an explicit open-question -> decision -> canon chain), domain-code (/decision + /lessons + /codify -> CLAUDE.md / task-rules), and this workspace itself (working notes -> AUDIT + decision records -> canon amendments). The spine is the PROMOTION DISCIPLINE: a working note (cheap, transient, unratified) graduates to a durable decision ('recorded so it isn't re-litigated' — append-only, ordered, ratified, with a stated basis) which may graduate to canon (the project's authoritative layer). The ONE genuinely per-project concern — WHERE a promoted decision lands (which authoritative file) and what the project's promotion criteria + decision categories are — lives in the project-owned .claude/notes-canon.md seam; the promote step HARD-STOPS if it is unfilled (guessing where a decision becomes law is the one inference the module refuses to make). Cross-references are SOFT: the kernel similarity-recall memory (reached via module.ingest /remember + the injected memory_store tool) is the retrieval-optimized SIBLING for facts recalled by meaning, NOT a dependency and NOT the same primitive; module.tasks (AUDIT.md linkage) and module.jobs (scheduled retro) are conveniences, present-if-mounted. HONEST SCOPE: v0.1.0 ships the spine (notes-rules.md), the specification (content/BUILD_PLAN.md), the seam template, and the ledger templates. The /note, /decide, /promote, and /notes skills are the authoring build phase, gated per BUILD_PLAN.md M-1..M-3 and deliberately held until a real second consumer declares the module in requires.elements[] (the module.tasks extract-after-proof precedent).
rasa.convergence: Distills the institutional-memory primitive found across the 2026-07-04 cross-vertical admin-primitive survey. Sole wave-2 candidate that cleared both the portfolio synthesis and the adversarial critic: genuinely convergent (three matching reimplementations, structurally identical three-tier lifecycle), zero kernel dependency, thin-but-real seam. See canon workspace CHANGELOG 2026-07-04 + memory universal-admin-primitives-and-wave2-verdict.

element.description: Element-owned files mirrored into the install target's .claude/ per canon ELEMENT_CONTRACT.md §7. Refreshed on Element upgrade. The notes-canon seam and the ledgers are NOT here — they are project-owned (see seed.files).

files.note: The portable institutional-memory spine: the three tiers (working / decision / canon), the promotion discipline and criteria, the boundary against the kernel's similarity-recall memory, append-only + ratification rules, and the ledger conventions. Element-owned; refreshed on upgrade. References .claude/notes-canon.md for the project's canon target + promotion policy; it hardcodes neither.
Describes what this module ships and how it installs. Fork/author-time doc; not installed into consumer projects.

The v0.1.0 specification + build plan (entity model, the three-tier pipeline, the four MVP skill contracts, the notes-canon seam, the kernel-memory boundary, deferred scope, the extract-after-proof gate). Author-time doc; not installed into consumer projects.

seed.description: One-time, project-owned files copied at install only (skip-if-exists), per canon ELEMENT_CONTRACT.md §7. These belong to the consuming project after install and are never overwritten on Element upgrade — the project's notes-canon adapter seam and its live ledgers.

files.note: The per-project adapter seam — the ONE thing that varies per vertical: where a promoted decision lands (the project's authoritative/canon file), the project's decision categories, and the promotion criteria (what makes a working note earn durable-decision status, and a decision earn canon status). Project-owned; the project fills it. /promote hard-stops until it is set. Ships with honest defaults + commented examples; placeholder-free per the documented skip-if-exists policy gap.

The durable decision-log ledger (tier 2) — append-only, ordered, one row per ratified decision with basis + category + date + optional promoted-to pointer. 'The project's institutional memory; recorded so it isn't re-litigated.' Project-owned; grows over the project's life.

The working-notes ledger (tier 1) — cheap, transient, unratified running notes (meeting notes, call notes, open threads, half-formed thoughts). Rolls to notes/archive/ when stale. Project-owned; distinct from durable decisions.

Stamped with the Element HEAD commit SHA at init time. Lockfile shape per canon Spec §6 (Connection Contract). When co-installed under a parent that also stamps a lockfile, the parent's is authoritative (single-writer-per-path, Lock L-004). Substitutes {{ELEMENT_NAME}}, {{ELEMENT_KIND}}, {{ELEMENT_VERSION}}, {{ELEMENT_REPO}}, {{ELEMENT_BRANCH}}, {{ELEMENT_SHA}}, {{TODAY}} via bin/init.

scaffold.description: Empty directories created at init by the declarative installer.
